behaviors.description: Embedding vectors have correct dimensionality matching the vector database index schema.
behaviors.justification: A real failure occurs when the embedding service returns vectors of unexpected dimensionality (e.g., model upgrade from 1536-dim to 768-dim vectors). Mocking the embedding service hides this because a mock can return any dimension. You must call the actual embedding API to verify its output dimensionality matches the database index requirements.
behaviors.test_description: Call the real embedding service with a test query, verify the returned vector has the expected dimension (e.g., 1536), and verify that inserting this vector into the production vector database schema succeeds without dimension constraint errors.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Testing against the real embedding service (System) is necessary; mocking at Unit level would miss actual API incompatibilities.

behaviors.description: Similarity search results are ordered by descending cosine similarity score.
behaviors.justification: A real failure occurs when the vector database returns results out of ranked order. Mocking the database would hide this (a mock can return results in any order). You need to interact with a real vector database instance populated with known test vectors, perform a query, and verify the ranking matches similarity scores. No external ML model behavior is being tested—only the database's ranking logic.
behaviors.test_description: Insert test vectors with known similarity distances into a test vector database (local Milvus instance or similar), perform a similarity search with a query vector, retrieve results, and assert they are ordered by descending cosine similarity score.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Testing against a real (or containerized) vector database is necessary; Unit mocks would bypass ranking verification.

behaviors.description: LLM re-ranking step changes result order compared to raw similarity ranking when query has nuanced intent.
behaviors.justification: A real failure occurs when the LLM fails to understand contextual intent or doesn't actually change the ranking. This depends on the real LLM's non-deterministic semantic understanding across different inputs and model versions. Mocking the LLM (which would trivially return any order) hides this failure. You must call the actual LLM with nuanced test queries to verify it produces meaningful re-rankings that reflect semantic understanding.
behaviors.test_description: Construct test cases with: (1) raw similarity-ranked results, (2) a query with nuanced or ambiguous intent (e.g., 'lightweight security tool' could mean different things), and (3) a call to the real LLM re-ranking function. Verify the LLM-produced ranking differs from the raw ranking and that differences align with the query's semantic intent.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: The actual LLM behavior is required; a mock would always pass but never verify real semantic re-ranking capability.

behaviors.description: Queries with no close matches return empty result set rather than low-relevance noise.
behaviors.justification: A real failure occurs when the similarity threshold filter fails to remove low-relevance results. Mocking the database would hide this if you only mock the happy path. You need a real vector database (or local test instance) with vectors semantically unrelated to the test query, perform the search, and verify the filtering logic correctly eliminates all results falling below the similarity threshold.
behaviors.test_description: Populate a test vector database with vectors unrelated to a test query (e.g., query='machine learning'; vectors='furniture', 'recipes', 'sports'). Perform the search and verify the result set is empty or filtered to zero results because all matches fall below the configured similarity threshold.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Testing against a real (or local) vector database with realistic similarity scores is necessary to verify threshold filtering behavior.

behaviors.description: End-to-end latency from query reception to re-ranked results is below 2 seconds (median case).
behaviors.justification: A real failure occurs when total latency (embedding call + vector search + LLM re-ranking + network round-trips) exceeds 2 seconds. Mocking these services would hide latency issues because mocks are fast in-process calls with no network overhead. You must call real external APIs (embedding service, vector database, LLM) over the actual network to measure genuine latency. This is a System-level requirement because it depends on real HTTP round-trips and external service response times.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the full API endpoint with real external services: make a real HTTP request with a query, measure the time from request receipt to response body, and ensure the median latency across multiple runs (with realistic query complexity and database size) stays below 2 seconds. Repeat enough times to verify median behavior, not just best-case.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Real services and actual network latency are required; any mocking of embedding, database, or LLM would report false-negative latency performance.
